text.Rotherham: Where the word of a king is, there is power,—who then may say to him, What wouldst thou do?
text.KJV: 8:4 Where the word of a king is, there is power: and who may say vnto him, What doest thou?
text.NABRE: 8:4 His word is sovereign, and who can say to him, “What are you doing?”

text.NRSV: Whoever keeps a command will know no evil thing, and the wise mind will know the time and way.
text.Rotherham: He that observeth the commandment, will not notice a vexatious thing,—and, of time and manner, will the heart of the wise take note.
text.KJV: 8:5 Whoso keepeth the commandement, shall feele no euill thing: and a wise mans heart discerneth both time and iudgement.
text.NABRE: 8:5 “Whoever observes a command knows no harm, and the wise heart knows times and judgments.”

text.NRSV: For every matter has its time and way, although the troubles of mortals lie heavy upon them.
text.Rotherham: For, to every pursuit, there is a time and a manner,—when, the vexation of man, is great concerning it.
text.KJV: 8:6 ¶ Because to euery purpose there is time, and iudgement; therefore the misery of man is great vpon him.
text.NABRE: 8:6 Yes, there is a time and a judgment for everything. But it is a great evil for mortals

text.NRSV: Indeed, they do not know what is to be, for who can tell them how it will be?
text.Rotherham: For there is no one who knoweth what shall be, for, when it shall be, who will tell him?
text.KJV: 8:7 For hee knoweth not that which shall be: for who can tell him, when it shall be?
text.NABRE: 8:7 that they are ignorant of what is to come; for who will make known to them how it will be?

text.NRSV: No one has power over the wind to restrain the wind, or power over the day of death; there is no discharge from the battle, nor does wickedness deliver those who practice it.
text.Rotherham: No man, hath power over the spirit, to retain the spirit, and, none, hath power over the day of death, and there is no furlough in war,—neither shall lawlessness deliver them who are given thereto.
text.KJV: 8:8 There is no man that hath power ouer the spirit to retaine the spirit; neither hath he power in the day of death: and there is no discharge in that warre, neither shall wickednesse deliuer those that are giuen to it.
text.NABRE: 8:8 No one is master of the breath of life so as to retain it, and none has mastery of the day of death. There is no exemption in wartime, nor does wickedness deliver those who practice it.

text.NRSV: All this I observed while applying my mind to all that is done under the sun, while one person exercises authority over another to the other’s hurt.
text.Rotherham: All this, had I seen, and tried to apply my heart to every work which was done under the sun,—at such time as one man had power over another man, to his hurt.
text.KJV: 8:9 All this haue I seene, and applied my heart vnto euery worke that is done vnder the Sunne: there is a time wherein one man ruleth ouer another to his owne hurt.
text.NABRE: 8:9 All these things I saw and I applied my heart to every work that is done under the sun, while one person tyrannizes over another for harm.

text.NRSV: Then I saw the wicked buried; they used to go in and out of the holy place, and were praised in the city where they had done such things. This also is vanity.
text.Rotherham: And, thereupon, I considered the lawless when buried, when they had entered, [their graves], that, from the place of the Holy One, they used to go and boast in the city that they had so done,—even this, was vanity.
text.KJV: 8:10 And so I saw the wicked buried, who had come, and gone from the place of the Holy, and they were forgotten in the city, where they had so done: this is also vanitie.
text.NABRE: 8:10 The Problem of Retribution. Meanwhile I saw the wicked buried. They would come and go from the holy place. But those were forgotten in the city who had acted justly. This also is vanity.

text.NRSV: Because sentence against an evil deed is not executed speedily, the human heart is fully set to do evil.
text.Rotherham: Because sentence against a wicked work is not executed speedily—on this account, the heart of the sons of men is fully set within them, to commit wickedness.
text.KJV: 8:11 Because sentence against an euill worke is not executed speedily; therefore the heart of the sonnes of men is fully set in them to doe euill.
text.NABRE: 8:11 Because the sentence against an evil deed is not promptly executed, the human heart is filled with the desire to commit evil—

text.NRSV: Though sinners do evil a hundred times and prolong their lives, yet I know that it will be well with those who fear God, because they stand in awe before him;
text.Rotherham: Though a sinner be committing wickedness a hundred times, and continuing long in his own way, yet I surely know that it shall be well to them who revere God, who stand in awe before him;
text.KJV: 8:12 ¶ Though a sinner doe euill an hundred times, and his dayes be prolonged; yet surely I know that it shall be well with them that feare God, which feare before him.
text.NABRE: 8:12 because the sinner does evil a hundred times and survives. Though indeed I know that it shall be well with those who fear God, for their reverence toward him;

text.NRSV: but it will not be well with the wicked, neither will they prolong their days like a shadow, because they do not stand in awe before God.
text.Rotherham: but, well, shall it not be to the lawless man, neither shall he lengthen out his days like a shadow,—because he standeth not in awe before God.
text.KJV: 8:13 But it shall not be well with the wicked, neither shall hee prolong his dayes which are as a shadow; because he feareth not before God.
text.NABRE: 8:13 and that it shall not be well with the wicked, who shall not prolong their shadowy days, for their lack of reverence toward God.

text.NRSV: There is a vanity that takes place on earth, that there are righteous people who are treated according to the conduct of the wicked, and there are wicked people who are treated according to the conduct of the righteous. I said that this also is vanity.
text.Rotherham: Here was a vain thing which was done upon the earth—that there were righteous men unto whom it happened according to the work of the lawless, and there were lawless men, unto whom it happened according to the work of the righteous,—I said, that, even this, was vanity.
text.KJV: 8:14 There is a vanitie which is done vpon the earth, that there be iust men vnto whom it happeneth according to the worke of the wicked: againe, there be wicked men, to whom it happeneth according to the worke of the righteous: I said, that this also is vanitie.
text.NABRE: 8:14 This is a vanity that occurs on earth: There are those who are just but are treated as though they had done evil, and those who are wicked but are treated as though they had done justly. This, too, I say is vanity.

text.NRSV: So I commend enjoyment, for there is nothing better for people under the sun than to eat and drink and enjoy themselves, for this will go with them in their toil through the days of life that God gives them under the sun.
text.Rotherham: Then extolled I, gladness, in that there was nothing better for a man, under the sun, than to eat and to drink, and to be glad,—since, that, should tarry with him in his toil, for the days of his life which God had given him under the sun.
text.KJV: 8:15 Then I commended mirth, because a man hath no better thing vnder the Sunne, then to eate and to drinke, and to be merrie: for that shall abide with him of his labour, the dayes of his life, which God giueth him vnder the Sunne.
text.NABRE: 8:15 Therefore I praised joy, because there is nothing better for mortals under the sun than to eat and to drink and to be joyful; this will accompany them in their toil through the limited days of life God gives them under the sun.

text.NRSV: When I applied my mind to know wisdom, and to see the business that is done on earth, how one’s eyes see sleep neither day nor night,
text.Rotherham: When I gave my heart, to know wisdom, and to consider the business that was done upon the earth, then surely, by day and by night, there was one who suffered not his eyes, to sleep.
text.KJV: 8:16 ¶ When I applied mine heart to know wisedome, and to see the busines that is done vpon the earth: (for also there is that neither day nor night seeth sleepe with his eyes.)
text.NABRE: 8:16 I applied my heart to know wisdom and to see the business that is done on earth, though neither by day nor by night do one’s eyes see sleep,

text.NRSV: then I saw all the work of God, that no one can find out what is happening under the sun. However much they may toil in searching, they will not find it out; even though those who are wise claim to know, they cannot find it out.
text.Rotherham: Then I considered all the work of God, that man could not find out the work that was done under the sun, inasmuch as man toileth in seeking and yet cannot find,—yea, even though the wise man should say he knoweth, yet can he not find it out.
text.KJV: 8:17 Then I behelde all the worke of God, that a man cannot finde out the worke that is done vnder the Sunne: because though a man labour to seeke it out, yea further though a wise man thinke to know it, yet shall hee not be able to finde it.
text.NABRE: 8:17 and I saw all the work of God: No mortal can find out the work that is done under the sun. However much mortals may toil in searching, no one finds it out; and even if the wise claim to know, they are unable to find it out.

text.NRSV: All this I laid to heart, examining it all, how the righteous and the wise and their deeds are in the hand of God; whether it is love or hate one does not know. Everything that confronts them
text.Rotherham: For, unto all this, I applied my heart, and, my heart, considered all this, that, the righteous and the wise and their servants, were in the hand of God,—neither love nor hatred, could any man know, every one, was before Him.
text.KJV: 9:1 For all this I considered in my heart, euen to declare all this, that the righteous, and the wise, and their workes, are in the hand of God: no man knoweth either loue, or hatred, by all that is before them.
text.NABRE: 9:1 B. No One Knows the Future Chapter 9 - All this I have kept in my heart and all this I examined: The just, the wise, and their deeds are in the hand of God. Love from hatred mortals cannot tell; both are before them.

text.NRSV: is the same for all, since one fate comes to all, to the righteous and the wicked, to the good and the evil, to the clean and the unclean, to those who sacrifice and those who do not sacrifice. As are the good, so are the sinners; and those who swear are like those who shun an oath.
text.Rotherham: Every one, was like every one else, one destiny, had the righteous and the lawless, the good and the pure and the impure, and he that sacrificed, and he that did not sacrifice,—as the good man, so, the sinner, he that took an oath, as he who, of an oath, stood in fear.
text.KJV: 9:2 All things come alike to all: there is one euent to the righteous and to the wicked, to the good and to the cleane, and to the vncleane; to him that sacrificeth, and to him that sacrificeth not: as is the good, so is the sinner, and hee that sweareth, as he that feareth an oath.
text.NABRE: 9:2 Everything is the same for everybody: the same lot for the just and the wicked, for the good, for the clean and the unclean, for the one who offers sacrifice and the one who does not. As it is for the good, so it is for the sinner; as it is for the one who takes an oath, so it is for the one who fears an oath.

text.NRSV: This is an evil in all that happens under the sun, that the same fate comes to everyone. Moreover, the hearts of all are full of evil; madness is in their hearts while they live, and after that they go to the dead.
text.Rotherham: This was a misfortune in all that was done under the sun, that, one destiny, had they all,—yea also, the heart of the sons of men, was full of wickedness, and, madness, was in their heart, while they lived, and, after that, [they went] unto the dead.
text.KJV: 9:3 This is an euill among all things that are done vnder the Sunne, that there is one euent vnto all: yea also the heart of the sonnes of men is full of euill, and madnesse is in their heart while they liue, and after that they goe to the dead.
text.NABRE: 9:3 Among all the things that are done under the sun, this is the worst, that there is one lot for all. Hence the hearts of human beings are filled with evil, and madness is in their hearts during life; and afterward—to the dead!

text.NRSV: But whoever is joined with all the living has hope, for a living dog is better than a dead lion.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever was united to all the living, for him, there was hope,—inasmuch as, a living dog, fared better than a dead lion.
text.KJV: 9:4 ¶ For to him that is ioyned to all the liuing, there is hope: for a liuing dogge is better then a dead Lion.
text.NABRE: 9:4 For whoever is chosen among all the living has hope: “A live dog is better off than a dead lion.”

text.NRSV: The living know that they will die, but the dead know nothing; they have no more reward, and even the memory of them is lost.
text.Rotherham: For, the living, knew that they should die,—but, the dead, knew not, anything, neither had they any longer a reward, because forgotten was their memory.
text.KJV: 9:5 For the liuing know that they shall die: but the dead know not any thing, neither haue they any more a reward, for the memorie of them is forgotten.
text.NABRE: 9:5 For the living know that they are to die, but the dead no longer know anything. There is no further recompense for them, because all memory of them is lost.

text.NRSV: Their love and their hate and their envy have already perished; never again will they have any share in all that happens under the sun.
text.Rotherham: Both their love and their hatred and their envy, already had perished,—and, portion, had they none any longer, unto times age-abiding, in aught that was done under the sun.
text.KJV: 9:6 Also their loue, and their hatred, and their enuy is now perished; neither haue they any more a portion for euer in any thing that is done vnder the Sunne.
text.NABRE: 9:6 For them, love and hatred and rivalry have long since perished. Never again will they have part in anything that is done under the sun.

text.NRSV: Go, eat your bread with enjoyment, and drink your wine with a merry heart; for God has long ago approved what you do.
text.Rotherham: Go thy way—eat, with gladness, thy food, and drink, with a happy heart, thy wine,—when already God is well pleased with thy works.
text.KJV: 9:7 ¶ Goe thy way, eate thy bread with ioy, and drinke thy wine with a merry heart; for God now accepteth thy workes.
text.NABRE: 9:7 Go, eat your bread with joy and drink your wine with a merry heart, because it is now that God favors your works.

text.NRSV: Let your garments always be white; do not let oil be lacking on your head.
text.Rotherham: Continually, let thy garments be white,—and, ointment upon thy head, let it not be lacking.
text.KJV: 9:8 Let thy garments bee alwayes white; and let thy head lacke no oyntment.
text.NABRE: 9:8 At all times let your garments be white, and spare not the perfume for your head.

text.NRSV: Enjoy life with the wife whom you love, all the days of your vain life that are given you under the sun, because that is your lot in life and in your toil at which you toil under the sun.
text.Rotherham: Enjoy life, with thy wife whom thou lovest, all the days of thy life of vanity, which he hath given thee under the sun, all thy days of vanity,—for, that, is thy portion in life, and in thy toil wherewith, thou, art toiling under the sun.
text.KJV: 9:9 Liue ioyfully with the wife, whom thou louest, all the dayes of the life of thy vanitie, which he hath giuen thee vnder the Sunne, all the dayes of thy vanitie: for that is thy portion in this life, and in thy labour which thou takest vnder the Sunne.
text.NABRE: 9:9 Enjoy life with the wife you love, all the days of the vain life granted you under the sun. This is your lot in life, for the toil of your labors under the sun.

text.NRSV: Whatever your hand finds to do, do with your might; for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol, to which you are going.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, with thy might, do,—for there is no work nor calculation nor knowledge nor wisdom, in hades, whither, thou, art going.
text.KJV: 9:10 Whatsoeuer thy hand findeth to doe, doe it with thy might: for there is no worke, nor deuice, nor knowledge, nor wisedome in the graue, whither thou goest.
text.NABRE: 9:10 Anything you can turn your hand to, do with what power you have; for there will be no work, no planning, no knowledge, no wisdom in Sheol where you are going.

text.NRSV: Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift, nor the battle to the strong, nor bread to the wise, nor riches to the intelligent, nor favor to the skillful; but time and chance happen to them all.
text.Rotherham: I again saw under the sun, that not, to the swift, was the race, nor, to the strong, the battle, nay! nor, to the wise, food, nor yet, to the intelligent, riches, nor, even to the well-informed, comeliness,—for, time and accident, happened to them all.
text.KJV: 9:11 ¶ I returned, and saw vnder the Sunne, That the race is not to the swift, nor the battell to the strong, neither yet bread to the wise, nor yet riches to men of vnderstanding, nor yet fauour to men of skil; but time and chance happeneth to them all.
text.NABRE: 9:11 The Time of Misfortune Is Not Known. Again I saw under the sun that the race is not won by the swift, nor the battle by the valiant, nor a livelihood by the wise, nor riches by the shrewd, nor favor by the experts; for a time of misfortune comes to all alike.

text.NRSV: For no one can anticipate the time of disaster. Like fish enumerated in a fatal net, and like birds caught in a snare, so mortals are snared at a time of calamity, when it suddenly falls upon them.
text.Rotherham: For, indeed, man could not know his own time, like fishes which were caught in a cruel net, and like little birds which were caught in a trap,—like them, were ensnared the sons of men, by a time of misfortune, when it fell upon them suddenly.
text.KJV: 9:12 For man also knoweth not his time, as the fishes that are taken in an euil net, and as the birds that are caught in the snare; so are the sonnes of men snared in an euill time, when it falleth suddenly vpon them.
text.NABRE: 9:12 Human beings no more know their own time than fish taken in the fatal net or birds trapped in the snare; like these, mortals are caught when an evil time suddenly falls upon them.

text.NRSV: I have also seen this example of wisdom under the sun, and it seemed great to me.
text.Rotherham: Even this, had I seen of wisdom, under the sun,—and, of great import, was the same unto me:—
text.KJV: 9:13 ¶ This wisedome haue I seene also vnder the Sunne, and it seemed great vnto me:
text.NABRE: 9:13 The Uncertain Future and the Sages. On the other hand I saw this wise deed under the sun, which I thought magnificent.

text.NRSV: There was a little city with few people in it. A great king came against it and besieged it, building great siegeworks against it.
text.Rotherham: A little city, and men therein few,—and there came against it a great king, and surrounded it, and built against it large siege-works;
text.KJV: 9:14 There was a little citie, and few men within it; and there came a great King against it, and besieged it, & built great bulwarks against it:
text.NABRE: 9:14 Against a small city with few inhabitants advanced a mighty king, who surrounded it and threw up great siegeworks about it.

text.NRSV: Now there was found in it a poor wise man, and he by his wisdom delivered the city. Yet no one remembered that poor man.
text.Rotherham: but there was found therein, a man, poor [but] wise, and, he, delivered the city by his wisdom,—yet, no one, remembered that poor man.
text.KJV: 9:15 Now there was found in it a poore wise man, and hee by his wisedome deliuered the citie; yet no man remembred that same poore man.
text.NABRE: 9:15 But in the city lived a man who, though poor, was wise, and he delivered it through his wisdom. Yet no one remembered this poor man.

text.NRSV: So I said, “Wisdom is better than might; yet the poor man’s wisdom is despised, and his words are not heeded.”
text.Rotherham: Then said, I, Better is wisdom than strength,—although, the wisdom of the poor man, be despised, and, his words, not heard.
text.KJV: 9:16 Then said I, Wisedome is better then strength: neuerthelesse, the poore mans wisedome is despised, and his words are not heard.
text.NABRE: 9:16 Though I had said, “Wisdom is better than force,” yet the wisdom of the poor man is despised and his words go unheeded.

text.NRSV: The quiet words of the wise are more to be heeded than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
text.Rotherham: The words of the wise, in quietness, are heard,—beyond the outcry of one who ruleth over dullards.
text.KJV: 9:17 The words of wise men are heard in quiet, more then the cry of him that ruleth among fooles.
text.NABRE: 9:17 The quiet words of the wise are better heeded than the shout of a ruler of fools.

text.NRSV: Wisdom is better than weapons of war, but one sinner destroys much good.
text.Rotherham: Better is wisdom, than weapons of war,—but, one sinner, may destroy much good.
text.KJV: 9:18 Wisedome is better then weapons of warre: but one sinner destroyeth much good.
text.NABRE: 9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war, but one bungler destroys much good.

text.NRSV: Dead flies make the perfumer’s ointment give off a fetid odor; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
text.Rotherham: Dead flies, cause to stink [and] ferment, the oil of the perfumer,—More costly than wisdom or honour, is a little folly.
text.KJV: 10:1 Dead flies cause the oyntment of the Apothecarie to send foorth a stinking sauour: so doeth a little folly him that is in reputation for wisedome and honour.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - Dead flies corrupt and spoil the perfumer’s oil; more weighty than wisdom or wealth is a little folly!

text.NRSV: The heart of the wise inclines to the right, but the heart of a fool to the left.
text.Rotherham: The sense of the wise, is on his right hand,—But, the sense of the dullard, on his left:
text.KJV: 10:2 A wise mans heart is at his right hand: but a fooles heart at his left.
text.NABRE: 10:2 The wise heart turns to the right; the foolish heart to the left.

text.NRSV: Even when fools walk along the road, they lack sense, and they show everyone that they are fools.
text.Rotherham: Yea, even by the way, as the foolish man walketh along, his sense faileth him—and he telleth everyone that, foolish, is he!
text.KJV: 10:3 Yea also when hee that is a foole walketh by the way, his wisedome faileth him, and hee saith to euery one that he is a foole.
text.NABRE: 10:3 Even when walking in the street the fool, lacking understanding, calls everyone a fool.

text.NRSV: If the anger of the ruler rises against you, do not leave your post, for calmness will lay great offenses to rest.
text.Rotherham: If, the spirit of a ruler, riseth up against thee, thy place, do not leave, for gentleness, pacifieth such as have greatly erred.
text.KJV: 10:4 If the spirit of the ruler rise vp against thee, leaue not thy place; for yeelding pacifieth great offences.
text.NABRE: 10:4 Should the anger of a ruler burst upon you, do not yield your place; for calmness abates great offenses.

text.NRSV: There is an evil that I have seen under the sun, as it were an error proceeding from the ruler:
text.Rotherham: Here was a misfortune I had seen under the sun,—a veritable mistake that was going forth from the presence of one who had power:
text.KJV: 10:5 There is an euill which I haue seene vnder the Sunne, as an errour, which proceedeth from the ruler.
text.NABRE: 10:5 I have seen under the sun another evil, like a mistake that proceeds from a tyrant:

text.NRSV: folly is set in many high places, and the rich sit in a low place.
text.Rotherham: Folly placed in great dignity,—while, the rich, in a low place, took their seat:
text.KJV: 10:6 Folly is set in great dignitie; and the rich sit in lowe place.
text.NABRE: 10:6 a fool put in high position, while the great and the rich sit in lowly places.

text.NRSV: I have seen slaves on horseback, and princes walking on foot like slaves.
text.Rotherham: I had seen, servants, upon horses,—and, rulers, walking like servants, on the ground.
text.KJV: 10:7 I haue seene seruants vpon horses, and princes walking as seruants vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: 10:7 I have seen slaves on horseback, while princes went on foot like slaves.

text.NRSV: Whoever digs a pit will fall into it; and whoever breaks through a wall will be bitten by a snake.
text.Rotherham: He that diggeth a pit, thereinto, may fall,—and, he that breaketh through a hedge, there may bite him a serpent.
text.KJV: 10:8 He that diggeth a pit, shall fall into it; and who so breaketh an hedge, a serpent shall bite him.
text.NABRE: 10:8 Whoever digs a pit may fall into it, and whoever breaks through a wall, a snake may bite.

text.NRSV: Whoever quarries stones will be hurt by them; and whoever splits logs will be endangered by them.
text.Rotherham: He that removeth stones, may be hurt therewith,—and he that cleaveth wood, may be endangered thereby.
text.KJV: 10:9 Who so remoueth stones, shall be hurt therewith: and hee that cleaueth wood, shalbe endangered thereby.
text.NABRE: 10:9 Whoever quarries stones may be hurt by them, and whoever chops wood is in danger from it.

text.NRSV: If the iron is blunt, and one does not whet the edge, then more strength must be exerted; but wisdom helps one to succeed.
text.Rotherham: If, blunt, be the iron, and, himself, hath not sharpened, the edge, then, much force, must he apply,—but, an advantage for giving success, is wisdom.
text.KJV: 10:10 If the yron be blunt, and he doe not whet the edge, then must he put to more strength: but wisedome is profitable to direct.
text.NABRE: 10:10 If the ax becomes dull, and the blade is not sharpened, then effort must be increased. But the advantage of wisdom is success.

text.NRSV: If the snake bites before it is charmed, there is no advantage in a charmer.
text.Rotherham: If a serpent will bite, unless he is charmed, then there is nothing better for him that owneth a tongue.
text.KJV: 10:11 Surely the serpent will bite without inchauntment, and a babbler is no better.
text.NABRE: 10:11 If the snake bites before it is charmed, then there is no advantage in a charmer.

text.NRSV: Words spoken by the wise bring them favor, but the lips of fools consume them.
text.Rotherham: The words of a wise man’s mouth, are pleasant,—but, the lips of a dullard, will swallow him up:
text.KJV: 10:12 The words of a wise mans mouth are gratious: but the lips of a foole will swallow vp himselfe.
text.NABRE: 10:12 Words from the mouth of the wise win favor, but the lips of fools consume them.

text.NRSV: The beginning of their words is folly, and the end of their talk is wicked madness;
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the words of his mouth, is folly,—but, the latter end of his speech, is mischievous madness.
text.KJV: 10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishnesse: and the end of his talke is mischieuous madnesse.
text.NABRE: 10:13 The beginning of their words is folly, and the end of their talk is utter madness;

text.NRSV: yet fools talk on and on. No one knows what is to happen, and who can tell them what will be after them?
text.Rotherham: Yet, a foolish man, multiplieth words,—[though] no man knoweth that which hath been, and, that which shall be after him, who can tell him?
text.KJV: 10:14 A foole also is full of words; a man cannot tell what shall be; and what shall bee after him who can tell him?
text.NABRE: 10:14 yet fools multiply words. No one knows what is to come, for who can tell anyone what will be?

text.NRSV: The toil of fools wears them out, for they do not even know the way to town.
text.Rotherham: The toil of dullards, shall weary a man, that he knoweth not how to go into the city.
text.KJV: 10:15 The labour of the foolish wearyeth euery one of them; because hee knoweth not how to goe to the citie.
text.NABRE: 10:15 The toil of fools wearies them, so they do not know even the way to town.

text.NRSV: Alas for you, O land, when your king is a lad, and your princes feast in the morning!
text.Rotherham: Alas! for thee, O land, when thy king is a boy,—and, thy rulers, in the morning, do eat:
text.KJV: 10:16 ¶ Woe to thee, O land, when thy king is a child, and thy princes eate in the morning.
text.NABRE: 10:16 No One Knows What Evil Will Come - Woe to you, O land, whose king is a youth, and whose princes feast in the morning!

text.NRSV: Happy are you, O land, when your king is a nobleman, and your princes feast at the proper time—for strength, and not for drunkenness!
text.Rotherham: How happy art thou, O land, when thy king is a son of nobles,—and, thy rulers, in season, do eat, for strength, and not for debauchery.
text.KJV: 10:17 Blessed art thou, O land, when thy king is the sonne of nobles, and thy princes eate in due season, for strength, and not for drunkennesse.
text.NABRE: 10:17 Happy are you, O land, whose king is of noble birth, and whose princes dine at the right time— for vigor and not in drinking bouts.

text.NRSV: Through sloth the roof sinks in, and through indolence the house leaks.
text.Rotherham: By two lazy arms, the framework sinketh in,—and, by the hanging down of the hands, the house may leak.
text.KJV: 10:18 ¶ By much slouthfulnesse the building decayeth; and through idlenesse of the hands the house droppeth through.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Because of laziness, the rafters sag; when hands are slack, the house leaks.

text.NRSV: Feasts are made for laughter; wine gladdens life, and money answers everything.
text.Rotherham: Merrily people make bread, and, wine, gladdeneth life,—but, money, answereth all things.
text.KJV: 10:19 ¶ A feast is made for laughter, and wine maketh merry: but money answereth all things.
text.NABRE: 10:19 A feast is made for merriment and wine gives joy to the living, but money answers for everything.

text.NRSV: Do not curse the king, even in your thoughts, or curse the rich, even in your bedroom; for a bird of the air may carry your voice, or some winged creature tell the matter.
text.Rotherham: Even in thy thought, do not revile, the king, nor, within thy bed-chambers, revile thou the rich,—for, a bird of the heavens, might carry the voice, yea, an owner of wings, might tell the matter.
text.KJV: 10:20 ¶ Curse not the king, no not in thy thought, and curse not the rich in thy bed-chamber: for a bird of the aire shall carry the voyce, and that which hath wings shall tell the matter.
text.NABRE: 10:20 Even in your thoughts do not curse the king, nor in the privacy of your bedroom curse the rich; For the birds of the air may carry your voice, a winged creature may tell what you say.

text.NRSV: Send out your bread upon the waters, for after many days you will get it back.
text.Rotherham: Cast thy bread-corn, upon the face of the waters,—for, after many days, shalt thou find it:
text.KJV: 11:1 Cast thy bread vpon the waters: for thou shalt find it after many dayes.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - Send forth your bread upon the face of the waters; after a long time you may find it again.

text.NRSV: Divide your means seven ways, or even eight, for you do not know what disaster may happen on earth.
text.Rotherham: Give a portion to seven, yea even to eight,—for thou canst not know, what there shall be of misfortune, upon the earth.
text.KJV: 11:2 Giue a portion to seuen and also to eight; for thou knowest noc what euill shall be vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: 11:2 Make seven, or even eight portions; you know not what misfortune may come upon the earth.

text.NRSV: When clouds are full, they empty rain on the earth; whether a tree falls to the south or to the north, in the place where the tree falls, there it will lie.
text.Rotherham: If the clouds be filled with a downpour, upon the earth, will they empty themselves, and, if a tree fall in the south or in the north, in the place where the tree falleth, there will it be found.
text.KJV: 11:3 If the clouds be full of raine, they emptie themselues vpon the earth: and if the tree fall toward the South, or toward the North, in the place where the tree falleth, there it shall be.
text.NABRE: 11:3 No One Knows What Good Will Come - When the clouds are full, they pour out rain upon the earth. Whether a tree falls to the south or to the north, wherever it falls, there shall it lie.

text.NRSV: Whoever watches the wind will not sow; and whoever looks at the clouds will not reap.
text.Rotherham: He that observeth the wind, will not sow,—and, he that watcheth the clouds, will not reap.
text.KJV: 11:4 He that obserueth the wind, shall not sow: and hee that regardeth the clouds, shall not reape.
text.NABRE: 11:4 One who pays heed to the wind will never sow, and one who watches the clouds will never reap.

text.NRSV: Just as you do not know how the breath comes to the bones in the mother’s womb, so you do not know the work of God, who makes everything.
text.Rotherham: Just as thou knowest not what is the way of the spirit, when the body is in the womb of her that is with child, even, so, canst thou not know the work of God, who maketh all.
text.KJV: 11:5 As thou knowest not what is the way of the spirit, nor how the bones doe growe in the wombe of her that is with child: euen so thou knowest not the workes of God who maketh all.
text.NABRE: 11:5 Just as you do not know how the life breath enters the human frame in the mother’s womb, So you do not know the work of God, who is working in everything.

text.NRSV: In the morning sow your seed, and at evening do not let your hands be idle; for you do not know which will prosper, this or that, or whether both alike will be good.
text.Rotherham: In the morning, sow thy seed, and, until evening, do not withhold thy hand,—for thou knowest not—whether shall thrive, either this or that, or whether, both alike, shall be fruitful.
text.KJV: 11:6 In the morning sowe thy seede, and in the euening withhold not thine hand: for thou knowest not whether shall prosper, either this or that, or whether they both shall be alike good.
text.NABRE: 11:6 In the morning sow your seed, and at evening do not let your hand be idle: For you do not know which of the two will be successful, or whether both alike will turn out well.

text.NRSV: Light is sweet, and it is pleasant for the eyes to see the sun.
text.Rotherham: Truly sweet is the light,—and, pleasant to the eyes, to see the sun:
text.KJV: 11:7 ¶ Truly the light is sweet, and a pleasant thing is it for the eyes to behold the sunne.
text.NABRE: 11:7 Poem on Youth and Old Age. Light is sweet! and it is pleasant for the eyes to see the sun.

text.NRSV: Even those who live many years should rejoice in them all; yet let them remember that the days of darkness will be many. All that comes is vanity.
text.Rotherham: But, though, many years, a man live, through them all, let him rejoice; yet let him remember the days of darkness, for many they may be, all that cometh, may be vanity.
text.KJV: 11:8 But if a man liue many yeeres, and reioyce in them all; yet let him remember the dayes of darkenesse, for they shall be many. All that commeth is vanitie.
text.NABRE: 11:8 However many years mortals may live, let them, as they enjoy them all, remember that the days of darkness will be many. All that is to come is vanity.

text.NRSV: Rejoice, young man, while you are young, and let your heart cheer you in the days of your youth. Follow the inclination of your heart and the desire of your eyes, but know that for all these things God will bring you into judgment.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth, and let thy heart gladden thee in the days of thine early manhood, and walk thou—in the ways of thine own heart, and in that which is seen by thine own eyes,—yet know, that, for all these things, will God bring thee into judgment.
text.KJV: 11:9 ¶ Reioyce, O young man, in thy youth, and let thy heart cheere thee in the dayes of thy youth, and walke in the wayes of thine heart, and in the sight of thine eyes: but know thou, that for all these things, God will bring thee into iudgement.
text.NABRE: 11:9 Rejoice, O youth, while you are young and let your heart be glad in the days of your youth. Follow the ways of your heart, the vision of your eyes; Yet understand regarding all this that God will bring you to judgment.

text.NRSV: Banish anxiety from your mind, and put away pain from your body; for youth and the dawn of life are vanity.
text.Rotherham: Therefore remove thou vexation from thy heart, and put away discomfort from thy flesh,—for, youth and dawn, are vanity!
text.KJV: 11:10 Therefore remoue sorrow from thy heart, and put away euill from thy flesh; for child-hood & youth are vanitie.
text.NABRE: 11:10 Banish misery from your heart and remove pain from your body, for youth and black hair are fleeting.

text.NRSV: Remember your creator in the days of your youth, before the days of trouble come, and the years draw near when you will say, “I have no pleasure in them”;
text.Rotherham: Yet remember thy Creator, in the days of thy vigour,—or ever come in, the days of discomfort, and the years arrive, in which thou shalt say—I have, in them, no pleasure;
text.KJV: 12:1 Remember now thy Creatour in the dayes of thy youth, while the euil daies come not, nor the yeeres drawe nigh, when thou shalt say, I haue no pleasure in them:
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 - Remember your Creator in the days of your youth, before the evil days come And the years approach of which you will say, “I have no pleasure in them”;

text.NRSV: before the sun and the light and the moon and the stars are darkened and the clouds return with the rain;
text.Rotherham: Or ever be darkened—the sun, and the light, and the moon, and the stars,—and the clouds return after a downpour of rain;
text.KJV: 12:2 While the Sunne, or the light, or the moone, or the starres be not darkened, nor the cloudes returne after the raine:
text.NABRE: 12:2 Before the sun is darkened and the light and the moon and the stars and the clouds return after the rain;

text.NRSV: in the day when the keepers of the house tremble, and the strong men are bent, and the women who grind cease because they are few, and those who look through the windows see dimly;
text.Rotherham: In the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble, and the men of might bow themselves,—and the grinders cease because they are few, and they who look through the windows are darkened;
text.KJV: 12:3 In the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble, and the strong men shall bowe themselues, and the grinders cease, because they are fewe, and those that looke out of the windowes be darkened:
text.NABRE: 12:3 When the guardians of the house tremble, and the strong men are bent; When the women who grind are idle because they are few, and those who look through the windows grow blind;

text.NRSV: when the doors on the street are shut, and the sound of the grinding is low, and one rises up at the voice of a bird, and all the daughters of song are brought low;
text.Rotherham: And the doors in the street be closed, when the sound of the mill become low,—and one rise at the chirp of a small bird, and low-voiced be all the daughters of song;
text.KJV: 12:4 And the doores shal be shut in the streets, when the sound of the grinding is low, and he shall rise vp at the voice of the bird, and all the daughters of musicke shall be brought low.
text.NABRE: 12:4 When the doors to the street are shut, and the sound of the mill is low; When one rises at the call of a bird, and all the daughters of song are quiet;

text.NRSV: when one is afraid of heights, and terrors are in the road; the almond tree blossoms, the grasshopper drags itself along and desire fails; because all must go to their eternal home, and the mourners go about the streets;
text.Rotherham: Yea, at what is high, they be in fear, and there be, terrors, in the way, and the almond be rejected, and the grasshopper drag itself along, and desire perish,—for man is going to his age-abiding home, when the wailers shall go round in the streets;
text.KJV: 12:5 Also when they shalbe afraid of that which is high, and feares shall bee in the way, and the Almond tree shall flourish, and the grashopper shall be a burden, and desire shall faile: because man goeth to his long home, and the mourners goe about the streets:
text.NABRE: 12:5 When one is afraid of heights, and perils in the street; When the almond tree blooms, and the locust grows sluggish and the caper berry is without effect, Because mortals go to their lasting home, and mourners go about the streets;

text.NRSV: before the silver cord is snapped, and the golden bowl is broken, and the pitcher is broken at the fountain, and the wheel broken at the cistern,
text.Rotherham: Or ever the silver cord be loosed, or the golden bowl be broken,—or the bucket by the fountain be shivered, or the wheel at the well be broken;
text.KJV: 12:6 Or euer the siluer corde be loosed, or the golden bowle be broken, or the pitcher be broken at the fountaine, or the wheele broken at the cisterne.
text.NABRE: 12:6 Before the silver cord is snapped and the golden bowl is broken, And the pitcher is shattered at the spring, and the pulley is broken at the well,

text.NRSV: and the dust returns to the earth as it was, and the breath returns to God who gave it.
text.Rotherham: And the dust return to the earth, as it was,—and, the spirit, return unto God, who gave it.
text.KJV: 12:7 Then shall the dust returne to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall returne vnto God who gaue it.
text.NABRE: 12:7 And the dust returns to the earth as it once was, and the life breath returns to God who gave it.

text.NRSV: Vanity of vanities, says the Teacher; all is vanity.
text.Rotherham: Vanity of vanities, saith the Proclaimer, all, is vanity.
text.KJV: 12:8 ¶ Uanitie of vanities (saith the preacher) all is vanitie.
text.NABRE: 12:8 Vanity of vanities, says Qoheleth, all things are vanity!

text.NRSV: Besides being wise, the Teacher also taught the people knowledge, weighing and studying and arranging many proverbs.
text.Rotherham: Besides that, the Proclaimer being wise,—still further taught knowledge unto the people, and weighed and searched, arranged proverbs in abundance.
text.KJV: 12:9 And moreouer because the preacher was wise, he still taught the people knowledge, yea he gaue good heed, and sought out, and set in order many prouerbes.
text.NABRE: 12:9 Epilogue. Besides being wise, Qoheleth taught the people knowledge, and weighed, scrutinized and arranged many proverbs.

text.NRSV: The Teacher sought to find pleasing words, and he wrote words of truth plainly.
text.Rotherham: The Proclaimer sought to find out words giving delight, and to note down rightly, the words of truth.
text.KJV: 12:10 The preacher sought to finde out acceptable words, and that which was written was vpright, euen wordes of trueth.
text.NABRE: 12:10 Qoheleth sought to find appropriate sayings, and to write down true sayings with precision.

text.NRSV: The sayings of the wise are like goads, and like nails firmly fixed are the collected sayings that are given by one shepherd.
text.Rotherham: The words of the wise, are as goads, yea, as driven nails, their well-ordered sayings,—given from one shepherd.
text.KJV: 12:11 The wordes of the wise are as goads, and as nailes fastened by the masters of assemblies, which are giuen from one shepheard.
text.NABRE: 12:11 The sayings of the wise are like goads; like fixed spikes are the collected sayings given by one shepherd.

text.NRSV: Of anything beyond these, my child, beware. Of making many books there is no end, and much study is a weariness of the flesh.
text.Rotherham: And besides, from them, my son, be admonished,—Of making many books, there is no end, and, much study, is a weariness of the flesh.
text.KJV: 12:12 And further, by these, my sonne, be admonished: of making many bookes there is no end, and much studie is a wearinesse of the flesh.
text.NABRE: 12:12 As to more than these, my son, beware. Of the making of many books there is no end, and in much study there is weariness for the flesh.

text.NRSV: The end of the matter; all has been heard. Fear God, and keep his commandments; for this is the whole duty of everyone.
text.Rotherham: The conclusion of the matter—the whole, let us hear,—Towards God, be reverent, and, his commandments, observe, for, this, [concerneth] all mankind.
text.KJV: 12:13 ¶ Let vs heare the conclusion of the whole matter: Feare God, and keepe his commandements, for this is the whole duetie of man.
text.NABRE: 12:13 The last word, when all is heard: Fear God and keep his commandments, for this concerns all humankind;

text.NRSV: For God will bring every deed into judgment, including every secret thing, whether good or evil.
text.Rotherham: For, every work, will God bring into judgment, with every hidden thing,—whether good, or evil.
text.KJV: 12:14 For God shal bring euery worke into iudgement, with euer secret thing, whether it bee good, or whether it bee euill.
text.NABRE: 12:14 because God will bring to judgment every work, with all its hidden qualities, whether good or bad.
